attacking_style: Pure flat sprinter who launches from 200-400m, often freelancing from rival wheels rather than relying on own leadout. Vulnerable to timing errors, frequently going too early on long finishing straights.
team_tactics: Relies heavily on dedicated leadout trains (Jumbo-Visma/BikeExchange), particularly effective when MØRKØV/RICHEZE deliver through final 1km. When leadouts collapse into chaos, shows strong wheel-finding ability to contest from mid-pack positioning.
terrain_preference: Pure flat sprints with technical, chaotic finishes where leadouts disintegrate. Completely neutralized by even moderate selective climbs (20km+ from finish) or rolling terrain with coordinated tempo. Can survive crosswind selections when in form.
vulnerability: Boxed in technical finishes (at least 5 instances), premature sprint launches in headwinds/long straights, and eliminated early on any categorized climbs. Crashes in final 1.5km positioning phase repeatedly. Targeted by rival teams via coordinated tempo on rolling roads.
key_patterns: Frequently boxed in final 300m despite strong positioning, losing wins from winning positions (7+ instances)
Timing errors: launches too early from 200-300m on fast finishes, fades in final meters (4 instances)
Crashes/barriers contact in final 1.5km positioning phase when contesting sprint setup (5 instances)
Exceptional early season form (February-March peak) but struggles to maintain through Grand Tours (2019 pattern)
Cannot survive selective climbs 20km+ from finish; targeted by tempo on rolling terrain (Dauphine 2022)
Elite bike throw and millimetric finishing ability when positioning clean, compensates for raw speed deficit
form_trajectory: neutral: 2023 shows podiums without leadout trains (elite craft remains) but repeated boxing/positioning errors and team coordination issues suggest declining consistency rather than speed. Return from ban successful (2022 win) but vulnerability to technical finishes persists.
